content.full_text:
August 14, 1986


Maggie Erickson
Maggie Erickson for Supervisor
1740 Loma Drive
Camarillo, CA 93010
                                        Re: FPPC Advice No I-86-252

Dear Ms. Erickson:

     Thank you for your letter dated July 22, 1986, in which you
inquired about a response to a previous letter you sent us.  The
previous letter was dated February 10, 1986.  We have checked our
mail logs, and have no record of receiving your letter dated
February 10, 1986.

     You asked whether the bank interest received from your
campaign funds may be used for personal purposes.

     California Elections Code Sections 12400, et seq. set out the
permissible uses of campaign funds by a candidate or committee.
The Fair Political Practices Commission does not have authority to
interpret the Elections Code and, therefore, we are unable to
provide you with an answer to your question.

     However, enclosed for your information is a copy of California
Attorney General Opinion No. 82-902, 65 Ops. Cal Atty. Gen. 588
which addresses this issue.  If you have additional questions
concerning the use of campaign funds, please call Ted Prim,
Assistant Attorney General, at (916) 324-5481.

     The Fair Political Practices Commission administers and
interprets the Political Reform Act of 1974.  The campaign
disclosure provisions of the Act are explained in the following
commission information manuals, which are enclosed for your
information:

     (1)  1986 Information Manual on the Campaign Disclosure
          Provisions of the Political Reform Act

     (2)  1986 Guide for Candidates and Treasurers

     (3)  1986 Recordkeeping Manual (Campaign Disclosure Provisions)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Maggie Erickson
August 14, 1986
Page 2


     If you have any questions concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act, please contact the
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Division Chief
                                        Technical Assistance & Analysis
                                        Division

JP:kt

SUPERVISOR MAGGIE

ERICKSON
...She makes a difference

CAMPAIGN CHAIRMAN
BOB PAULSEN

CAMPAIGN CHAIRMAN
FRANK WHITE

TREASURER
JEAN RUDDY

July 22, 1986

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento,CA 95814

Gentlemen:

On February 10, 1986, I wrote requesting information with
respect to my interest-bearing campaign checking account. To
date I have not received a reply from you.

A copy of the February letter is attached hereto. Would you
please provide me with a response or direct my letter to the
proper agency for answer.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

MAGGIE ERICKSON

MAGGIE ERICKSON FOR SUPERVISOR · ID #791873 · 1740 Loma Drive, Camarillo, CA 93010

sections.question: Whether bank interest received from campaign funds may be used for personal purposes.
sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission does not have authority to interpret the Elections Code and, therefore, we are unable to provide you with an answer to your question. However, enclosed for your information is a copy of California Attorney General Opinion No. 82-902, 65 Ops. Cal Atty. Gen. 588 which addresses this issue.
sections.question_synthetic: Can bank interest earned from campaign funds be used for personal purposes by a candidate?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks authority to interpret the Elections Code on this issue and declined to provide a direct answer. Instead, the FPPC referred the requester to the California Attorney General Opinion No. 82-902 and directed them to contact the Assistant Attorney General for further guidance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: FPPC response with no formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers; question and conclusion extracted from letter body. Document includes both the FPPC response (August 14, 1986) and the incoming request (July 22, 1986). FPPC issued a declination of advice authority.

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission does not have authority to interpret the Elections Code and, therefore, we are unable to provide you with an answer to your question. However, enclosed for your information is a copy of California Attorney General Opinion No. 82-902, 65 Ops. Cal Atty. Gen. 588 which addresses this issue.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to Maggie Erickson's inquiry about the permissible use of bank interest from campaign funds, explaining that it lacks jurisdiction over Elections Code interpretation and referring her to relevant Attorney General guidance and the Technical Assistance division.
